questions: redis ki cache hishtabe use hoy?
redis vs memcached which is better?
answers: Redis is an in-memory data store used as a cache, message broker, and database. It's incredibly fast because it keeps all data in RAM and supports various data structures like strings, lists, sets, and hashes.
Redis is the world's most popular in-memory data store. It's used for caching, session management, real-time leaderboards, pub/sub messaging, and rate limiting with sub-millisecond response times.

Redis stores data in memory for blazing-fast reads and writes. It supports persistence to disk, replication, and clustering. Its data structures like sorted sets and streams make it far more than a simple cache.
Redis is essential for high-performance applications. Use it for caching database queries, managing user sessions, building real-time features, and as a message broker between microservices.
